result.summary: The page has a few visual defects: a header button with no visible label, and a 'SCROLL DOWN' element overlapping the legal terms text at the bottom of the hero section, making the disclosure text partially unreadable. The fine print itself is also very small and low-contrast in places.

issues.description: The yellow button in the top-right of the header is completely empty — it has no text label or icon, so users cannot tell what it does (likely intended to be a 'Sign Up' or 'Log In' CTA).
issues.suggestion: Ensure the button's label text renders (e.g., 'Join Now' or 'Log In'). Check for a failed text load, missing localization string, or CSS hiding the label.

issues.description: The 'SCROLL DOWN' indicator with chevrons overlaps the terms & conditions fine print at the bottom of the promo section, obscuring several lines of legally required disclosure text.
issues.suggestion: Reposition the scroll indicator below the T&C paragraph or add spacing/z-index/background handling so the legal text remains fully legible.

issues.description: The terms & conditions text over the yellow background uses very small blue/white text with poor contrast in places, making the mandatory promotional disclosures hard to read.
issues.suggestion: Increase the font size of the legal copy and ensure text color meets WCAG AA contrast against the yellow background.

issues.description: The 'SCROLL DOWN' pill has a semi-transparent/white box that abruptly cuts through the text block, creating a visually broken, unpolished appearance.
issues.suggestion: Give the scroll indicator its own dedicated row with clear separation from surrounding content instead of floating it over text.
